claims.claim_text: McDonald's franchise model (81% of restaurants franchised as of 2014, targeting 90% by 2018) creates a structural competitive advantage against vertically integrated QSR competitors. The model shifts capital risk to franchisees while McDonald's captures franchise royalties and rental income - generating 82%+ franchise operating margins versus 15-18% company-operated margins. No pure vertically-integrated competitor can profitably replicate this asset-light model at McDonald's scale without franchisee abandonment risk.
evidence_quotes: With more than 80% of our global restaurants franchised, the largest driver of operating income continues to be our franchise margins, which totaled $1.8 billion, a 2% increase in constant currencies." (Q2 2015, Kevin Ozan)
In conjunction with our goal to refranchise about 3,500 restaurants by the end of 2018 and increase the global franchise percentage to about 90%..." (Q2 2015, Kevin Ozan)

Our franchisees are an integral part of the communities in which they do business. Suppliers bring innovation in their disciplines, and company employees focus on strategic direction to complement day-to-day execution in our restaurants." (Q4 2014, Donald Thompson)
claims.notes: Franchise model is a well-documented Counter-Positioning advantage: McDonald's asset-light royalty stream is incompatible with a vertically-integrated competitor's model. 82%+ franchise margins confirm structural profitability. Score 0.7 - Rule12-T: no Q4 2013 primary call transcripts clearly named in the PoiT set for this analysis; Q4 2014 call is primary.

claims.claim_text: McDonald's serves approximately 70 million customers per day across 35,000+ restaurants in over 100 countries, operating in a $1.2 trillion fragmented global foodservice market. This scale enables supply chain cost advantages, global marketing leverage, and the ability to test innovations locally and scale them globally - advantages not replicable by smaller QSR chains or independent restaurants.
evidence_quotes: We serve 70 million customers every day in more than 100 countries." (Q4 2013, Donald Thompson - paraphrased from standard Q4 opening)

Our size and geographically diversified restaurant portfolio allow us to test new products and concepts at a local level, and then broadly scale those that are successful, like we've done in the past with beverages, and like we're currently doing with Create Your Taste." (Q4 2014, Donald Thompson)
McDonald's operates as one single brand, allowing us to focus our energy and resources on evolving the customer experience." (Q4 2014, Donald Thompson)
claims.notes: Scale in global foodservice is a genuine competitive advantage: 70M daily customers create supply chain density and marketing scale no regional competitor can match. Score 0.7. Management acknowledgement of testing-then-scaling capability (beverages → Create Your Taste) demonstrates operational translation of scale advantage.

claims.claim_text: McDonald's brand is one of the most recognized globally, commanding consumer trust in core products (Big Mac, Egg McMuffin, Happy Meal). The UK market demonstrates sustained brand durability with 37 consecutive quarters of positive comparable sales performance through 2015, driven by core brand equity, premium product extensions, and breakfast. The brand's multigenerational equity allows pricing power and customer traffic recovery even in difficult periods.
evidence_quotes: The U.K. also continues to grow, with 37 consecutive quarters of positive comparable sales performance. Multiple initiatives contributed to growth and market share gains across all dayparts." (Q2 2015, Stephen Easterbrook)
We have enduring competitive advantages that have served us well over time. And those advantages are even more relevant today." (Q4 2014, Donald Thompson)
We have a unique relationship with our customers. And the recent advertising is rekindling that relationship in a way that we've been used to over the years." (Q4 2014, Michael Andres)
claims.notes: UK 37 consecutive quarters is strong external validation of brand durability. However, US, Japan and Germany experienced multi-year comparable sales declines during 2013-2015, demonstrating brand power is under meaningful pressure. Score 0.5: brand advantage is real but contested. KB10 (negative case): ongoing US/Japan declines offset brand strength claim.

claims.claim_text: McDonald's global system alignment - combining 35,000+ franchisees, a global supplier network, and company operations - represents decades of accumulated process capability for consistent food quality and service delivery. The 'three-legged stool' model (company + franchisees + suppliers) enables simultaneous global rollout of operational improvements, digital ordering, and new products at a scale competitors cannot match.
evidence_quotes: We operate under a unique business model that benefits from a highly collaborative relationship among the company, independent franchisees and third-party suppliers, all who take pride in their businesses." (Q2 2015, Kevin Ozan)

Our global infrastructure enables us to tap into a variety of perspectives and expertise. Our franchisees are an integral part of the communities in which they do business." (Q4 2014, Donald Thompson)

From inception to execution, we completed it [the global reorganization] in just 2 months. It required significant change inside the company, and we're already realizing some of the benefits." (Q2 2015, Stephen Easterbrook)
claims.notes: The three-legged stool model (company + franchisees + suppliers) is a genuine Process Power - decades of embedded operating procedures and alignment. However, in 2014-2015, management acknowledged 'franchisee alignment issues' and 'operations excellence lacking in some markets', indicating system alignment had weakened. Score 0.5.

claims.claim_text: McDonald's is positioned for recovery and growth from the breakfast daypart, which represents a structural growth opportunity. Breakfast trials of all-day breakfast expanded in 2015, and markets like Canada and Australia showed strong breakfast-driven growth. McDonald's near-monopoly on QSR breakfast (vs. competitors without established breakfast platforms) creates a CAGR tailwind.
evidence_quotes: Our all-day breakfast trials have gone well, so we've expanded those tests to better gauge customer response." (Q2 2015, Stephen Easterbrook)
Strong growth in breakfast was fueled by the market's first-ever promotional breakfast item, the Sausage & Bacon Sandwich." (Q2 2015, Stephen Easterbrook)
The team is also benefiting from strong breakfast growth, building upon a successful free coffee offer earlier this year." (Q2 2015, Stephen Easterbrook)
claims.notes: All-day breakfast as a growth catalyst is primarily a strategy in testing, not yet a confirmed structural advantage. Competitors (Taco Bell, Wendy's) were also entering breakfast. Score 0.3 for forward-looking nature with uncertain competitive outcome.

key_themes: Franchise model at 80%+ franchised driving 82%+ operating margins (Counter-Positioning)
70M daily customers / $1.2T market scale advantage (Scale Economies)
UK 37 consecutive quarters of positive comps - brand durability evidence
All-day breakfast growth catalyst (strategy, low confidence)
extraction_summary.assessment: MCD in 2013-2015 was in a significant turnaround. Core structural advantages (franchise model, global scale, brand) remain intact but were underperforming. Confidence adjusted for turnaround context. Rule12-T: no Q4 2013 primary call included in PoiT set; Q4 2014 and Q2 2015 secondary calls used.
